mnemonics: CON-CAT: CONnect two lists like linking CATs' tails together
keyPoints: Concatenation joins two lists end-to-end to create a new combined list
The original lists remain unchanged unless explicitly reassigned
Most programming languages provide built-in functions or operators (like '+' in Python) for list concatenation
commonMistakes: Modifying the original lists expecting them to change (concatenation typically returns a new list)
Confusing concatenation with appending (appending adds elements to one list, concatenation combines two lists)
Assuming concatenation works the same way for all data structure types across different languages
